---
title: "Research"
---

## Working Papers

::: {.paper}
### Farmer Willingness to Participate in Voluntary Conservation Programs: Evidence from Eastern North Carolina
::: {.coauthors}
with Greg Howard
:::

Uses discrete choice analysis to identify the economic and behavioral factors that drive farmer participation in voluntary conservation programs in Eastern North Carolina.

::: {.links}
[In Preparation]{.badge .badge-wp}
:::
:::

::: {.paper}
### Conflict Among Slope Ski Tourers in the Alps: Applications for Machine Learning
::: {.coauthors}
with Klaus Moeltner and Martin Schnitzer
:::

Applies machine learning methods to study conflict between ski tourers on shared Alpine slopes, with implications for recreation management and policy.

::: {.links}
[In Preparation]{.badge .badge-wp}
:::
:::

::: {.paper}
### Environmental Stress and Labor-Market Constraints on International Migration

Examines how environmental stress interacts with labor-market conditions at origin to shape international migration flows, using bilateral panel data across 138 countries.

::: {.links}
[In Preparation]{.badge .badge-wp}
:::
:::

::: {.paper}
### Warnings from the Web: Using Online Media Data to Anticipate Crop Conditions
::: {.coauthors}
with Mario Ortez, Michael Smith, and Nicole Widmar
:::

Uses natural language processing of online media to construct early-warning indicators of adverse crop conditions.

::: {.links}
[Under Review]{.badge .badge-wp}
:::
:::

::: {.paper}
### A Replicable Framework for Ex-Ante Property Value Impact Assessment of Proposed Utility-Scale Solar Facilities
::: {.coauthors}
with Zhenshan Chen
:::

Develops a replicable framework for projecting property value impacts of proposed utility-scale solar facilities before construction, combining matched comparable sites, difference-in-differences estimation, and welfare translation.

::: {.links}
[In Preparation]{.badge .badge-wp}
:::
:::

## Publications

::: {.paper}
### Integrating human decision-making into a hydrological model to accurately estimate the impacts of agricultural policies
::: {.coauthors}
with M.R. Tapas, Greg Howard, R. Etheridge, and A. Peralta. *Communications Earth & Environment* 6, 412 (2025).
:::
::: {.links}
[[Published Version]{.btn .btn-primary .btn-sm}](https://doi.org/10.1038/s43247-025-02325-x)
:::
:::

::: {.paper}
### Framework for Stakeholder-Driven Socio-Hydrological Modeling: Conceptual Foundations for Policy Development and Evaluation to Improve Ecosystem Health
::: {.coauthors}
with M.R. Tapas, R. Etheridge, and Greg Howard. In: Pandey, M., Umamahesh, N., Das, J., Pu, J.H. (eds) *Hydrology and Hydrologic Modelling. HYDRO 2023. Lecture Notes in Civil Engineering*, vol 410. Springer (2025).
:::
::: {.links}
[[Published Version]{.btn .btn-primary .btn-sm}](https://doi.org/10.1007/978-981-97-7474-6_42)
:::
:::
